showtitle: Return of the Curse of Timeland
shownotes: This show was billed as Return of the Curse of Timeland (a rescheduling of their cancelled 2021 New Year's Eve Timeland Festival) and featured the debuts of Magenta Mountain, The Garden Goblin, The Dripping Tap, and Soy-Protein Munt Machine. Timeland was played over the PA as the pre-show music. A model of London’s Big Ben clock tower, approximately 20 ft (6 m) tall, with the words “Time is Ephemeral” and “Music is Ephemeral” written on the clock’s face was erected in the back right corner of the stage.  The clock hands ran forward at an accelerated pace throughout the show.  Am I in Heaven? contained Rattlesnake teases and Boogieman Sam quotes/teases. Evil Death Roll contained Robot Stop teases. Trapdoor was played for the first time known since 2016-09-09. (259 show gap) The Dripping Tap was introduced as a song about a tap that won’t stop dripping.  Smoke & Mirrors was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Hot Wax was played for the first time since 2018-06-20. (114 show gap) Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs. Rattlesnake contained Boogieman Sam quotes and featured another Cavs drum solo, during which the band destroyed the clock tower presiding over the stage and passed out the pieces into the crowd. The on-stage carnage appears to have extended to some of the band’s equipment, as the final portion of Rattlesnake was performed with Joey playing the FMB and Stu singing with no instrument.
opener: Babe Rainbow, Stonefield, Ajak Kwai, Nice Biscuit, Bones & Jones w/Folk BItch Trio

The clock hands ran forward at an accelerated pace throughout the show.  Am I in Heaven? contained Rattlesnake teases and Boogieman Sam quotes/teases. Evil Death Roll contained Robot Stop teases. Trapdoor was played for the first time known since 2016-09-09. (259 show gap) The Dripping Tap was introduced as a song about a tap that won’t stop dripping.  Smoke & Mirrors was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Hot Wax was played for the first time since 2018-06-20. (114 show gap) Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs. Rattlesnake contained Boogieman Sam quotes and featured another Cavs drum solo, during which the band destroyed the clock tower presiding over the stage and passed out the pieces into the crowd. The on-stage carnage appears to have extended to some of the band’s equipment, as the final portion of Rattlesnake was performed with Joey playing the FMB and Stu singing with no instrument.
opener: Babe Rainbow, Stonefield, Ajak Kwai, Nice Biscuit, Bones & Jones w/Folk BItch Trio

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Blame It On the Weather. I’m Not In Your Mind contained Rattlesnake teases and Boogieman Sam quotes. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs. Mystery Jack was introduced as the oldest song that would be played that night, followed by a tease of “Frankenstein” (Edgar Winter Group). Mystery Jack was played for the first time since 2018-11-24 (86 show gap). Ataraxia was on the written setlist but was replaced by Doom City. Following O.N.E. opening band Body Type was shouted out. The Dripping Tap featured a “flute jam” duet between Stu and Cecil Coleman of Body Type and contained Pill teases and Cellophane teases/quotes.

jamchart_notes: Still in its infancy, this "Tap" bears witness to an inspired Stu dueting on flute with Cecil Coleman (Body Type) for a little "flute jam." Some nice harmonic interplay and Am I In Heaven?-style vamping ensues before a bevy of proper teases and quotes in the customary energetic run up to the coda.

I’m Not In Your Mind and Sea of Trees contained The Dripping Tap teases. Gaia featured a drum solo. Work This Time, which featured a guitar solo, was dedicated to Joey’s partner Edith (Amby’s sister), who briefly appeared on stage.  The Dripping Tap was introduced by Amby as a song he wrote about his kitchen sink and contained Cellophane teases.   Straws in the Wind was dedicated to Cavs' nipples.  Joey shouted out Mildlife following Straws.  Rattlesnake contained O.N.E. teases/quotes, Automation teases and See Me quotes. See Me and O.N.E. were on the setlist before Rattlesnake but were cut for time.

This was a fully acoustic show, the first one performed by the band outside of Australia. The River contained Wah Wah teases. Muddy Water featured Ambrose on sax. Cookie and Ambrose played on the same piano during Mr. Beat. Blame It On The Weather was introduced by Ambrose as a song about the weather in Melbourne, as well as being introduced as a song about Ambrose's dog by Stu. In the intro to The Bitter Boogie, which also featured Ambrose on sax, Joey said that the show is a "beta test" and asked if any of the crowd are going to Coachella, which the band played two days later. Dirt and Hot Water featured Mildlife guitarist Adam Halliwell on flute. Before Dirt, Joey quoted a scene from "School of Rock". The solo in Let Me Mend The Past was ommitted. Her and I featured Mildlife drummer Jim Rindfleish on congas and contained a Sleepwalker quote and tease and Crumbling Castle teases. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but only partial recordings exist. All songs listed were confirmed through audio/video recordings, however The River through Muddy Water and Most Of What I Like are only partially available so any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

showtitle: Coachella Valley Music And Arts Festival
shownotes: This show was part of the 2022 Coachella Festival, held at the Empire Polo Grounds in Indio, CA.  The band played their set in spite of heavy winds and audio issues (including hearing Harry Styles in their in-ear monitors, which Joey stated was fine with him but not Stu), which may have prompted Joey’s exclamation at the start of the show.  Self-Immolate was introduced as a song about “lighting yourself on fire and running like a fucking nutbag.”  Gaia was introduced as a song about “all the funky weather going on”, then as a song about drones followed by a statement by Stu about how much he loves drones and technology in general, and featured a drum solo by Cavs.  Gamma Knife was introduced as a song about shanks.
soundcheck: Gaia, The Dripping Tap, Pleura, Honey

shownotes: Nuclear Fusion was dedicated to Skrillex. Stu did the vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion at Joey’s request. SPELLLING and Amyl and the Sniffers were shouted out after Plastic Boogie. Her and I contained Iron Lung quotes and Slow Jam 1 quotes. Evil Death Roll contained Alter Me teases and quotes. Boogieman Sam was dedicated to Belgium (home of sound tech Gaspard Demulemeester) and contained Cut Throat Boogie quotes. Amby introduced The Dripping Tap as a song about his water bill.
venuename: Event Lawn at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas

shownotes: The show began with a chant of “fuck shit up” by the crowd, which was repeated several times throughout the show. Self-Immolate was introduced as a song about setting yourself on fire. Before starting Magenta Mountain the crowd’s chant was changed to “ma-gen-ta.” Slow Jam 1 contained Iron Lung quotes. Supreme Ascendancy was introduced as a song about “how much Amby loves our lord and savior…Satan!” SPELLLING and The Chats were shouted out after Oddlife. Following Ontology Stu and the audience had a discussion of the apple that Stu was eating during the show. The Lord of Lightning contained a Cellophane tease. Before Dead-Beat, Stu announced that they would be playing an older song. This announcement prompted several requests from the audience, including God is in the Rhythm, “Taste of an Orange” (Almacknjack) and “Stairway to Heaven” (Led Zeppelin) which was then teased by Joey, who then claimed that he had no idea how to play that song and referred to it as Stairway to Kevin. Am I in Heaven? contained Her and I (Slow Jam 2) teases, God Is Calling Me Back Home teases, The Dripping Tap teases/quotes, Boogieman Sam teases/quotes, Rattlesnake teases, Hot Wax quotes and Head On/Pill teases/quotes. KGLW.net Staff Notes: Special shout out to our very own @spidzzle for the recording located on Archive.org!
venuename: Event Lawn at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas

Am I in Heaven? contained Her and I (Slow Jam 2) teases, God Is Calling Me Back Home teases, The Dripping Tap teases/quotes, Boogieman Sam teases/quotes, Rattlesnake teases, Hot Wax quotes and Head On/Pill teases/quotes. shownotes: The show opened with an expression of disdain for the visiting New Orleans Pelicans of the NBA, who defeated the Phoenix Suns in a first-round playoff game earlier in the evening. Planet B was started and stopped several times before the full song was played. Honey contained Doom City teases. Sleep Drifter contained Billabong Valley quotes. The Bitter Boogie contained Presumptuous quotes and featured Stu feeding chips and salsa to the band and throwing chips out to the audience.

showtitle: Coachella Valley Music And Arts Festival
shownotes: The MOTU Suite was introduced as being about self-replicating robots turning the universe into vomit.  Following Predator X fellow festival acts Amyl & the Sniffers and The Chats were shouted out.  Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs.

Robot Stop contained Hot Water teases.  Gamma Knife contained People-Vultures teases. Self-Immolate featured a drum solo by Cavs.  Her and I contained an Am I In Heaven? tease, Iron Lung quotes/teases, Slow Jam 1 quotes, Ice V teases and The River teases.

Superbug was introduced as a "song we wrote in 2019 about 2020". Wah Wah contained teases and quotes of The River. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. Stevie Ray Vaughn, Jimi Hendrix, Steve Vai, John Butler, Joe Satriani, John Mayer and Korn were shouted out after Work This Time. Open Water featured a drum solo by Cavs and a Static Electricity tease. Straws in the Wind contained The Grim Reaper quotes. O.N.E. was introduced as "about being on tour". Boogieman Sam was dedicated to Sam, the band's sound tech, and contained quotes from Hate Dancin' and “La Grange” (ZZ Top). The Dripping Tap contained a Hell's Itch jam with lyrics and Hot Water teases.
soundcheck: Evilest Man

jamchart_notes: A super punchy and energetic version. Having achieved its now-trademark blistering speed, they further push the boundaries of the budding jam vehicle and take an offramp to (a then-unknown) Hell's Itch jam. A vocally inspired "drip drip" section gives way to the hottest "Tap" peak yet. Cavs is on fire!

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Persistence.  Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs.  Honey contained Sleep Drifter teases.  Cavs and Cookie performed the Nuclear Fusion intro together as a "duet." The Lord of Lightning was on the written setlist after K.G.L.W. (Outro) but was not played.

jamchart_notes:
Does “biggest show gap in years” count?

Crumbling Castle had been played literally every couple shows for years. Then the band took a little break from playing it; this is its triumphant return (after Covid paused live music), as the encore for a tiny punk venue in northern California.  (2nd Biggest gap for CC, after 2017-11-18)
shownotes: This show featured the debut of Persistence.  Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs.  Honey contained Sleep Drifter teases.  Cavs and Cookie performed the Nuclear Fusion intro together as a "duet." The Lord of Lightning was on the written setlist after K.G.L.W. (Outro) but was not played.

jamchart_notes: A near 5 minutes jam between Mr. Beat->Sea of Trees sets up a 10+ minute fully jammed out version of this song, one of the oldest in their discography.

Robot Stop had teases of Hot Water and People-Vultures. Before The Dripping Tap, Joey shouted out Trent Reznor of Nine Inch Nails, calling him "talented and hot", and Ambrose quoted "The Stroke" (Billy Squier). People-Vultures was on the printed setlist after Gamma Knife but not played.

Honey had teases from Billabong Valley and Sleep Drifter. Boogieman Sam had Cut Throat Boogie quotes. The Dripping Tap had I'm in your Mind quotes. After Honey, Stu says "give us that Tool beat", then says he was thinking of the wrong song and asks for that "Ginger Baker beat". Tezeta was played for the first time since 2019-10-07. (54 show gap) During Boogieman Sam, Stu was throwing some kind of food into the crowd, feeding it to the band, as well as tossing it in the air and catching it in his mouth.

3 hour marathon set. Before the show, "Timeland" was played on the speakers. The intro to Nuclear Fusion was done by the band's sound tech Gaspard. Mr. Beat contained a Persistence tease. Billabong Valley contained a Sleep Drifter tease. During the setbreak, "Smoke & Mirrors" was also played via the venue's speakers. I'm Not In Your Mind contained a Dripping Tap tease. Gaia featured a drum solo. Cellophane featured Stu on flute. The River was dedicated to "Ambrose's ass" and the second segment was played faster per Stu's request. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. Before The Dripping Tap, Stu shouted out the band's crew. The Dripping Tap contained Iron Lung quotes. Mystery Jack was on the setlist after The River, but not played due to time constraints.

jamchart_notes: Coming right out of Mars For The Rich, the band takes no time going straight into an intro jam. As soon as the flute comes out the band "heats up" and fires into an intense version of the track, with the last half featuring some especially great flute trills from Stu. Right as the track begins to wind down, they take a left turn when Stu swaps to guitar, entering an ambient outro which prompts Joey to pick up the flute himself.

shownotes: Lonely Steel Sheet Flyer was played for the first time since 12-11-17 (178 show gap) and had Ambrose on sax. The Dripping Tap was dedicated to "the moms". "Let's Go Crazy" (Prince) was quoted before The Dripping Tap. The Bitter Boogie featured "Sexual Healing" (Marvin Gaye) quotes, as well as Ambrose on sax. Evil Death Roll was on the setlist after I'm In Your Mind Fuzz, but not performed. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Lonely Steel Sheet Flyer, The Dripping Tap, and The Bitter Boogie have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the physical written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

I'm Not In Your Mind contained Am I In Heaven? and The Dripping Tap teases. The Balrog was played for the first time since 2019-10-16 (50 show gap). Wah Wah contained The River teases and Big Fig Wasp teases and quotes. Boogieman Sam contained Cut Throat Boogie quotes, Cyboogie quotes, “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole) quotes, “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes, “My Babe” (Little Walter) quotes, and a "La Grange" (ZZ Top) tease, as well as Stu on flute. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. This show featured the debut of Sadie Sorceress.  Robot Stop contained Hot Water teases.  Sleep Drifter contained Honey, Rattlesnake and Automation teases. Honey contained Sleep Drifter and Automation teases. Planet B was introduced as a song about capitalism. Han-Tyumi’s vocals in the MOTU suite were performed in Spanish.

This show featured the debut of both Presumptuous and The Grim Reaper. Her and I contained an Am I In Heaven? intro, Presumptuous quotes, and an Iron Lung jam with lyrics. Presumptuous was introduced as being "like what Santana sounds like in 2022." Sea of Trees contained alternate lyrics ("ignore it" instead of "unfold") and The Dripping Tap and Head On/Pill teases. Hot Water contained Shanghai teases. The Grim Reaper was introduced as a song about a time Ambrose's neighbor believed that devils were chasing her.

jamchart_notes: Coming in from Sea of Trees, it seems like just a tease of Hot Water followed by a tease of Shanghai, but Ambrose's chants of "Hot Water" turn it into a chilled jam, that increases in energy halfway though, like a pot of boiling… hot water.

Superbug (lyric changed from “H1N1” to “Coronavirus” referencing COVID-19). Inner Cell was dedicated to the former Australian Prime Minister Scott Morrison, who had lost an election that day. Inner Cell was played for the first time since 2019-10-15 (54 show gap), and Loyalty and Horology were played for the first time since 2019-10-18 (52 show gap). Cut Throat Boogie featured Boogieman Sam teases and quotes, Change teases, “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole) quotes, and “My Babe” (Little Walter) quotes. Persistence featured Change teases and Horology teases. Am I in Heaven? featured Cut Throat Boogie, Hot Water, and Change teases and The Dripping Tap, Iron Lung, Cellophane, Persistence, Sea of Trees, Magenta Mountain, and Ice V teases and quotes. The Lord of Lightning was on the printed setlist after Magenta Mountain but was not played.
soundcheck: (possibly incomplete) Inner Cell, Loyalty, Magenta Mountain, Mars for the Rich

shownotes:
Before Blame It On the Weather, Stu said they would play Vegemite next, but then clarified he was joking. After Blame It On The Weather, Stu tells a story of Joey sleepwalking and relieving himself on his new modular keyboard. Leading Joey to joke his new DJ name was Urine Man. The Garden Goblin was dedicated to Urine Man. During it, there was technical difficulty during which Stu discussed his accent. After The Garden Goblin, Joey says the song is about Cook’s dog, which Cook interjects, “yeah I miss him!” Work This Time featured a guitar solo. The Book featured a drum solo. Open Water was introduced as "one of our many songs about water" and also featured a drum solo. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam.

Poster By Daniel Herwitt @sunflower_form
soundcheck: O.N.E.; Predator X; Sadie Sorceress; The Garden Goblin; The Book; Cranes, Planes, Migraines

jamchart_notes: Beginning with a peaceful calm typical of the song, the band moves toward an evil jam which will become typical in future FAFYLs. They methodically open a portal leading to a relentless and pummeling descent into hell filled with tight, crunchy and melodic metal guitars, apocalyptic synthwork, and raging harmonica. After about five minutes of face melting delirium, the band suddenly falls away back into the original mellow groove, all the while incorporating and playing with themes from the metal section.

After Blame It On The Weather, Stu tells a story of Joey sleepwalking and relieving himself on his new modular keyboard. Leading Joey to joke his new DJ name was Urine Man. The Garden Goblin was dedicated to Urine Man. During it, there was technical difficulty during which Stu discussed his accent. After The Garden Goblin, Joey says the song is about Cook’s dog, which Cook interjects, “yeah I miss him!” Work This Time featured a guitar solo. The Book featured a drum solo. Open Water was introduced as "one of our many songs about water" and also featured a drum solo. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam.

Poster By Daniel Herwitt @sunflower_form
soundcheck: O.N.E.; Predator X; Sadie Sorceress; The Garden Goblin; The Book; Cranes, Planes, Migraines

shownotes: Before The Dripping Tap, Joey chanted "Turn it up Sammy". I'm In Your Mind was introduced as a song about getting a new haircut, followed by a short riff being played. I'm Not In Your Mind contained Rattlesnake and Am I In Heaven? teases. Evil Death Roll contained Lord of Lightning, The Dripping Tap, Converge, and Shanghai teases. Down The Sink was played for the first time since 2019-10-16 (55 show gap). Rattlesnake contained Minimum Brain Size, Automation, O.N.E, Nuclear Fusion, Sleep Drifter, Honey, and Static Electricity teases and quotes. During the Honey tease the crowed began chanting "Honey".
soundcheck: Down the Sink, Presumptuous, The Grim Reaper, The Balrog

shownotes: Honey started with a Sleep Drifter intro and contained Sleep Drifter, Nuclear Fusion, and Billabong Valley teases. The River contained Iron Lung teases. Before Gamma Knife, Ambrose quoted "That's not a knife… THAT's a knife." from Crocodile Dundee and the song was introduced as a song about the future of knives. The Bitter Boogie featured Ambrose on sax. The Lord Of Lightning was on the printed setlist before The Bitter Boogie but was not played.

jamchart_notes: Spanning 20+ minutes, it contains an extended intro, killer lengthy harmonica jamming from Ambrose, sax jamming and amazing vocal work from Ambrose as well, along with a high energy ending.

This show featured the debut of Tetrachromacy. Following Self-Immolate, someone was kicked out of the venue. Persistence contained Head On/Pill teases and quotes. Muddy Water contained Perihelion teases and quotes and featured Ambrose on sax. Head On/Pill contained The Dripping Tap, Persistence, Alter Me, and Blame It On The Weather teases and quotes, as well as Shanghai and Change teases.

Poster By Michael Jager.

jamchart_notes: Perihelion teases and quotes
shownotes:
This show featured the debut of Tetrachromacy. Following Self-Immolate, someone was kicked out of the venue. Persistence contained Head On/Pill teases and quotes. Muddy Water contained Perihelion teases and quotes and featured Ambrose on sax. Head On/Pill contained The Dripping Tap, Persistence, Alter Me, and Blame It On The Weather teases and quotes, as well as Shanghai and Change teases.

Poster By Michael Jager.

jamchart_notes: Longest ever uninterrupted single King Gizzard song performance to date, wild amount of teases (including Change).
shownotes:

jamchart_notes: Super chill funky version starting off with a drum machine jam by Joey and ending in a guitar solo.
shownotes:
Cavs was unable to play due to getting COVID before the show. The band played with a drum machine with a Cavs shirt draped on top. Joey mentioned they would have gotten a fan on the drums if not for COVID restrictions. Sense opened with Joey doing jamming on the drum machine for a few minutes before the band came on and began playing Sense. Let Me Mend The Past featured Joey on acoustic guitar. Sleepwalker, Mystery Jack, and Presumptuous featured Cookie on acoustic guitar. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. Sea of Trees contained a Head On/Pill tease and a Change tease. 

Poster By B.Montero
soundcheck: Let Me Mend The Past, Rattlesnake, Hot Water, and Work This Time

shownotes: 2nd show with Cavs unable to play due to getting COVID. The band played with a drum machine, this time with no CAVS shirt. Magenta Mountain opened with a drum machine jam by Stu. Sadie Sorceress contained a lengthened intro where the band and crowd shouted Cavs. After Most Of What I Like, the band joked they would play Organ Farmer and Gaia with the drum machine next. Slow Jam 1 contained Her and I (Slow Jam 2) teases and Iron Lung quotes and teases. O.N.E. contained a Honey tease. Muddy Water and Float Along Fill Your Lungs were on the printed setlist at the end, but were cut.

This was the first of five nights in Barcelona for Primavera Sound. Honey contained Sleep Drifter and Rattlesnake teases. The printed setlist contained a different song order - 'Self-Immolate' first and 'Mars for the Rich' second. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Gaia through Honey, Magenta Mountain, and The Dripping Tap have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the physical written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This was the second of five nights in Barcelona for Primavera Sound. Stu’s Gibson Holy Explorer and Joey’s Gibson Flying V were crushed by a lift before the show. Mr. Beat contained Iron Lung teases. Wah Wah contained The River quotes. Only Part I and II of The River were performed. The Lord of Lightning was on the printed setlist but was not played. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. The Mind Fuzz Suite, People-Vultures, Blame It On The Weather, Hell, Plastic Boogie, and The Book have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

The songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show was part of the 2022 Bonnaroo Music & Arts Festival and was held in “That Tent”. Robot Stop was played as an homage to the live debut of Robot Stop at Bonnaroo in 2015 and contained Hot Water teases. Following Planet B, Joey shouted out Tool, who was also playing at the festival (but on a different day to his chagrin) and then teased The Grudge (Tool). Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs. Magenta Mountain contained Crumbling Castle teases. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. The Dripping Tap contained Dazed and Confused (Led Zeppelin) teases. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live at Bonnaroo ‘22.

I'm Not In Your Mind contained Magenta Mountain teases. Ambergris was dedicated to the band's crew member Joe Santarpia.  The Lord of Lightning was on the printed setlist but not played, with Joey joking before Trapdoor that they only put Lord of Lightning on a setlist whenever there's a storm close to an outdoor show and it gets played if the storm hits, then he shouted out opener Jess Cornelius. Boogieman Sam contained I'm Not A Man Unless I Have A Woman, The Dripping Tap, “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole), “My Babe” (Little Walter) and “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes and Cut Throat Boogie teases.

Poster By Daniel @sunflower_form

The Lord of Lightning was on the printed setlist but not played, with Joey joking before Trapdoor that they only put Lord of Lightning on a setlist whenever there's a storm close to an outdoor show and it gets played if the storm hits, then he shouted out opener Jess Cornelius. Boogieman Sam contained I'm Not A Man Unless I Have A Woman, The Dripping Tap, “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole), “My Babe” (Little Walter) and “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes and Cut Throat Boogie teases.

This show was the final show of the US leg of the band's 2022 spring/summer world tour. The River was dedicated to Michael Bay and contained Wah Wah teases. After The River, the band shouted out their crew. Sadie Sorceress featured a guitar solo and contained quotes from The Grim Reaper.

Converge (Demo) (or as it was known at the time, Uncolonize) played through the PA as the band walked on stage. Planet B was dedicated to the nearby wildfires. Honey contained Sleep Drifter teases. The Garden Goblin was introduced by Stu as a song Cookie wrote about Lucas, before Cookie clarifies that its about his dog in his garden. There were PA issues during Pleura and the show was paused midway through The Garden Goblin when the PA went down. During the pause the band reminisced about Radiohead's sound cutting out at Coachella 2017, a festival King Gizzard also performed at. Grace Cummings was thanked before Blame It On The Weather. Robot Stop contained Ice V quotes and Hot Water teases. Boogieman Sam contained Cut Throat Boogie quotes and teases. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Gaia through Robot Stop have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete or incorrect. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Converge (Demo) (or as it was known at the time, Uncolonize) played through the PA as the band walked on stage. Planet B was dedicated to the nearby wildfires. Honey contained Sleep Drifter teases. The Garden Goblin was introduced by Stu as a song Cookie wrote about Lucas, before Cookie clarifies that its about his dog in his garden. There were PA issues during Pleura and the show was paused midway through The Garden Goblin when the PA went down. During the pause the band reminisced about Radiohead's sound cutting out at Coachella 2017, a festival King Gizzard also performed at. Grace Cummings was thanked before Blame It On The Weather. Robot Stop contained Ice V quotes and Hot Water teases. Boogieman Sam contained Cut Throat Boogie quotes and teases. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Gaia through Robot Stop have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete or incorrect. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Raining Blood (Slayer) played before and as the band came on stage. The River [1] contained Wah Wah teases and parts I, II, & III were played before transitioning to Wah Wah, which contained The River teases and quotes. Part IV of the The River was played after Road Train. Ambergris was introduced as having slap bass. After Ambergris, Joey shouts out opener Grace Cummings. Slow Jam 1 contained an Iron Lung vocal jam and Witchcraft teases. Ataraxia was introduced as "Tanaraxia", which was explained to be a song about Joey wanting to be tan. Sleep Drifter contained a Doom City and Billabong Valley tease. Float Along – Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. Poster by Bjenny Montero Edition of 100

This show was part of the 2022 Desert Daze Festival (held on Moreno Beach at the Lake Perris State Recreation Area) and featured the live debut of Iron Lung. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo. Boogieman Sam contained Cut Throat Boogie, “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole) quotes, and “My Babe” (Little Walter) quotes. Following Murder of the Universe the band shouted out Tame Impala, who also appeared at the festival. Following K.G.L.W. (Outro), Joey called out “our other best friends Mild High Club”, who were also playing that night, and Ambrose got them confused with Mildlife, another group the band was friends with playing the festival the next day. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs had a false start due to Stu's guitar being out of tune and contained a metal jam. The Dripping Tap was introduced as a song “about swimming in a sea of death”, and during the song Stu crowd surfed to and swam in Lake Perris, then crowd surfed back to the stage. The song contained Torture Chamber quotes.

(Outro), Joey called out “our other best friends Mild High Club”, who were also playing that night, and Ambrose got them confused with Mildlife, another group the band was friends with playing the festival the next day. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs had a false start due to Stu's guitar being out of tune and contained a metal jam. The Dripping Tap was introduced as a song “about swimming in a sea of death”, and during the song Stu crowd surfed to and swam in Lake Perris, then crowd surfed back to the stage. The song contained Torture Chamber quotes.

jamchart_notes: A more melodious jam than the Best Kept Secret “Tap” accompanies Stu’s (now customary?) Lake Perris plunge. Amby rips some harmonica, improvises some lyrics, and then implores the crowd: “Can we have our singer back on the stage?” It would be a mistake to dismiss this fun, funny, and creative version as a mere retread!

This show featured the debut of Ice V. Wah Wah began with the intro to The River, and subsequently contained Crumbling Castle and The River teases. Ambrose quoted It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) before Doom City. O.N.E. began with the intro to Straws in the Wind instead of the customary intro. Pleura contained a quote of “Freedom” (Rage Against the Machine). Straws in the Wind was dedicated to Russell Brand. Smoke and Mirrors was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo. Ambergris contained an Ice V tease. Before The Reticent Raconteur, Leah Senior was introduced to the stage as “The Original Balrog” and Joey gave a shoutout to her live bass player Jack [Robbins], with the bonus trivia that he was also in Stu’s first band Almacknjack. The Reticent Raconteur was played for the first time since 2021-02-24 (61 show gap). The Reticent Raconteur and The Lord of Lightning featured narration by Leah Senior. The Fourth Colour was played for the first time since 2019-11-23 (70 show gap). Am I in Heaven? contained The Dripping Tap teases. Poster by Jason Galea 800 standard / 50 Foil Print

jamchart_notes: Highly improvisational and groovy, the jam starts out with percussive guitar work which morphs into some Ice V teasing and Iron Lung quotes. The jam moves into a psychedelic fury which quickly chills out and gets real spacey as Stu picks up the flute. Building up from a guitar, drums and flute trio, a heavy rocking and fast paced jam emerges, which simply rages. After a few minutes, Cavs shifts to a half time beat which transitions smoothly back into the song. “Well that took an unexpected turn” says Joey.

jamchart_notes: A bloodcurdling scream, a hypertensive first jam, a hellish itch, and a Pill to cure all ills; this show-closing jam vessel travels far and wide with its bodily imagery. The Gizzy Boys’ rhythm section lock a tight grip on “drip drip” while Stu, Amby, and Joey rip (especially during the coda jam, holy “Tap”)!

This was an in-studio appearance for radio station KEXP in Seattle. Following Magma the band had a 10 minute interview with KEXP DJ/on-air events coordinator Cheryl Waters.

An I In Heaven? was teased prior to Magenta Mountain. The intro to O.N.E. contained Straws in the Wind teases. The River contained Wah Wah teases and quotes throughout, as well as Crumbling Castle teases. The Land Before Timeland was played over the PA as the setbreak music, which incidentally served as the “debut” for the studio track. Rattlesnake contained teases and quotes from O.N.E., Automation, Honey, and Minimum Brain Size, and teases of Sleep Drifter. Honey contained teases of Sleep Drifter and Billabong Valley. The Reticent Raconteur through The Balrog featured Leah Senior (introduced as “The Balrog” and “the best singer”) providing the narration. Ambrose quoted “Happy Birthday, Lisa” (The Simpsons) while referencing Joey’s “Australian birthday” prior to The Grim Reaper. The Grim Reaper was then introduced as “some weird-ass Satanic rap”. Venusian 2 and Am I in Heaven? were on the printed setlist but were replaced with Planet B due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea This is the first edition of this print

Rattlesnake contained teases and quotes from O.N.E., Automation, Honey, and Minimum Brain Size, and teases of Sleep Drifter. Honey contained teases of Sleep Drifter and Billabong Valley. The Reticent Raconteur through The Balrog featured Leah Senior (introduced as “The Balrog” and “the best singer”) providing the narration. Ambrose quoted “Happy Birthday, Lisa” (The Simpsons) while referencing Joey’s “Australian birthday” prior to The Grim Reaper. The Grim Reaper was then introduced as “some weird-ass Satanic rap”. Venusian 2 and Am I in Heaven? were on the printed setlist but were replaced with Planet B due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea This is the first edition of this print

Stu and Ambrose wished Joey a happy birthday during The Dripping Tap. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. Oddlife was introduced as “Cookie’s favorite song”. Boogieman Sam was dedicated to soundman Sam Joseph and contained Cut Throat Boogie teases/quotes, “My Babe” (Little Walter), “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole), and “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes and Sleepwalker teases. Sea of Trees was introduced as “what King Gizzard was originally called” and “Lucas’s favorite song”, and contained Head On/Pill and The Bitter Boogie teases. Hypertension was played over the PA as the setbreak music, which incidentally served as the “debut” for the studio track. I’m Not in Your Mind contained Alter Me teases. A New World through Altered Beast III featured Leah Senior providing narration. Alter Me I contained an Altered Beast III tease. Alter Me II and Altered Beast III were played for the first time since 2019-10-07 (90 show gap). The intro to Iron Lung featured additional vocals about “hot buns”. Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease. Iron Lung was reprised during Mr. Beat. Joey was hit with silly string at the conclusion of the show, and Ambrose quoted “Happy Birthday, Lisa” (The Simpsons) for the second night in a row. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea

Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease. Iron Lung was reprised during Mr. Beat. Joey was hit with silly string at the conclusion of the show, and Ambrose quoted “Happy Birthday, Lisa” (The Simpsons) for the second night in a row. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea

Stu and Ambrose wished Joey a happy birthday during The Dripping Tap. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. Oddlife was introduced as “Cookie’s favorite song”. Boogieman Sam was dedicated to soundman Sam Joseph and contained Cut Throat Boogie teases/quotes, “My Babe” (Little Walter), “Got My Mojo Working” (Ann Cole), and “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes and Sleepwalker teases. Sea of Trees was introduced as “what King Gizzard was originally called” and “Lucas’s favorite song”, and contained Head On/Pill and The Bitter Boogie teases. Alter Me II and Altered Beast III were played for the first time since 2019-10-07 (90 show gap). The intro to Iron Lung featured additional vocals about “hot buns”. Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease. Iron Lung was reprised during Mr. Beat. Joey was hit with silly string at the conclusion of the show, and Ambrose quoted “Happy Birthday, Lisa” (The Simpsons) for the second night in a row. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea

This show featured the debut of Lava. Slow Jam 1 contained Iron Lung quotes. The Book was introduced as a song by Jimi Hendrix and featured a Cavs drum solo. The Reticent Raconteur through The Balrog featured narration by Leah Senior. The Lord of Lightning contained a Cellophane tease. The Balrog contained a Trapdoor tease. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam.

The start of the show was delayed by 4-5 minutes due to issues with Stu’s guitar/equipment. There was much humorous banter from the band during the delay. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. The segue from O.N.E. to Sleep Drifter contained a Honey tease. Let Me Mend The Past contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote. Only Parts I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained an Am I In Heaven? tease and a Wah Wah tease. The River [Part III] was reprised during Wah Wah, which also contained a quote of The River. Poster By Carlo Vivary

Only Parts I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained an Am I In Heaven? tease and a Wah Wah tease. The River [Part III] was reprised during Wah Wah, which also contained a quote of The River. Poster By Carlo Vivary

The show opened with a brief drum solo by Cavs prior to the rest of the band taking the stage. History owner (Drake) (“Champagne Papi”) was mentioned a number of times over the course of the show. Rattlesnake contained O.N.E. and Automation quotes and teases. Ambrose crowd surfed while singing the refrain in Straws in the Wind. Joey introduced Anoxia as “my Neil Young impersonation song.” Ice V was introduced as being “in memory of Queen E.” Prior to Digital Black, Joey said "hello" numerous times in a Han-Tyumi impression. Soy-Protein Munt Machine and Vomit Coffin contained Hypertension teases. The Dripping Tap contained an I’m in Your Mind Fuzz Suite tease. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The recordings of this show are incomplete (missing Digital Black). KGLW.net staff do have enough to confirm the > into Han-Tyumi and that Ice V came to a full stop. For the show opening drum solo, it was discussed whether this should be added to the setlist as “Fury” in order to match with the show opening drum solo from the Melbourne ‘21 bootlegger release. It was decided that the drum solos labeled “Fury” by the band consist of a distinct double bass drum part which is absent from this drum solo and that other drum solos have been given different names (i.e. “Parking” on Chunky Shrapnel), therefore it was decided to leave this drum solo as a show note since we do not have definitive information on what it should be called. If anyone has any further information about these items, or ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Carlo Vivary

This show featured the debut of Red Smoke.  Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. Leah Senior was shouted out after Hot Water.  The intro of Robot Stop contained Big Fig Wasp teases. Hot Water was reprised during Robot Stop. Work This Time featured a guitar solo.

Poster By Carlo Vivary

Honey contained Sleep Drifter and Rattlesnake teases. The Sleep Drifter outro -> to O.N.E. contained Billabong Valley teases. Leah Senior and Black Midi were shouted out after O.N.E. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band, which also contained People-Vultures teases. After the solo, Joey lamented that they didn’t have another drum set to hold a drum battle between Cavs and Morgan of Black Midi. Iron Lung contained Hypertension teases.

Posters By Jason Galea
Show: 555 Standard / 55 Foil Print
Record Fair: 100 Standard Print
soundcheck: Magenta Mountain w/ attempted > The Grim Reaper

Honey contained Sleep Drifter and Rattlesnake teases. The Sleep Drifter outro -> to O.N.E. contained Billabong Valley teases. Leah Senior and Black Midi were shouted out after O.N.E. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band, which also contained People-Vultures teases. After the solo, Joey lamented that they didn’t have another drum set to hold a drum battle between Cavs and Morgan of Black Midi. Iron Lung contained Hypertension teases.

shownotes:
Her and I contained Hell's Itch teases.  After The Fourth Colour, Joey asks the crowd to “take a step back”. The Grim Reaper contained Sadie Sorceress quotes. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were played for the first time since 2019-10-08 (96 show gap). Evil Death Roll contained Alter Me teases, Hell's Itch teases and a Hypertension Jam, which would debut properly the following night.

Poster By Arash Zarifian
Standard 250 / 30 Foil Prints

jamchart_notes: The band slithers towards a Hypertension jam that transforms into Altered Beast, then emerges with a clear Hypertension tease. After Invisible Face, they boom back into EDR to a raucous close complete with spooky background vocals.

This show featured the live debut of Hypertension. The Reticent Raconteur and The Lord of Lightning were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. I’m in Your Mind contained Cellophane teases. Cellophane contained Alter Me teases and quotes. Self-Immolate featured a Cavs drum solo which led into a brief metal vamp and ultimately returned to Self-Immolate. Rattlesnake contained Automation and Nuclear Fusion teases. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam.

The show began with a drum solo leading into Venusian 2. Before Predator X, an “Asheville Gatherum” shirt was thrown on stage, and Stu changed into it. Slow Jam 1 contained Ice V and Magenta Mountain teases. Magenta Mountain was dedicated to Robert “Bob” Moog, inventor of the Moog synthesizer. Nuclear Fusion featured Lucas on the intro vocal. Another t-shirt was thrown up on stage after Iron Lung; Stu thanked everyone for the t-shirts noting that “I fucking stink like shit so it’s perfect.” After Stu declared that “showers are for squares,” Ambrose then suggested that Stu had never had a shower in his life, “only golden ones.” Wah Wah contained a The River intro, as well as teases and quotes. The Dripping Tap contained The Land Before Timeland teases.

Poster By Lewy Jones

jamchart_notes: The third of three monstrous October renditions sees the band grooving through Ice V teasing, before moving onto mystical and atmospheric passages, including a great breakdown jam at the 10 minute mark. The keys shine particularly bright in the back half of the jam. The band builds the energy back up to briefly return for a final refrain, before floating back to outer space for a little Magenta Mountain jam to close out.

Another t-shirt was thrown up on stage after Iron Lung; Stu thanked everyone for the t-shirts noting that “I fucking stink like shit so it’s perfect.” After Stu declared that “showers are for squares,” Ambrose then suggested that Stu had never had a shower in his life, “only golden ones.” Wah Wah contained a The River intro, as well as teases and quotes. The Dripping Tap contained The Land Before Timeland teases.

Poster By Lewy Jones

jamchart_notes: Though not its usual immaculate, tight self, the Asheville “Tap” finds the band stretching out into new territory and mapping out “The Land Before Timeland”.

It was announced prior to the show that Stu was late to the stage because he had “diarrhea or something”. Stu then entered the stage costumed as “Disco Stu” and noted that it was his birthday show. After Hell, Stu teased "Stayin' Alive" (Bee Gees). Persistence was stopped partway through for a cake “being fucked by a sexy alien” to be presented to Stu, and for the crowd and band to sing “Happy Birthday to You” to Stu, after which Stu passed out the cake to the audience and several jokes were made about the cake's contents. Persistence was finished after the mid-song break. The River contained Wah Wah teases and an Ice V tease. Leah Senior was shouted out before Lava. At the end of the show, Stu was hit with silly string by the band while the audience sang to him again. KGLW.net Staff Notes: Fans of stage banter will want to listen to this show for humorous birthday comments throughout the show, and other amusing banter about being drunk and the Satanic nuns that often appear at their shows. Poster By Josh Freydkis

Evil Death Roll did not include the Invisible Face section and instead featured a Hypertension-esque jam (see below). The intro to Ambergris featured musical banter regarding a “big-ass beer”. Leah Senior was shouted out prior to Crumbling Castle. KGLW.net Staff Notes: There was a good deal of discussion about whether the jam in Evil Death Roll constituted a Hypertension tease or was merely suggesting a variation on Hypertension through Stu’s playing. We decided that this example did not qualify as a “tease”, “quote”, or full “jam.” Rather than expand our lexicon to include “motif” or “theme,” we opted to simply mention and qualify it in the setlist notes. Poster By Jordan Speer

Hot Wax was announced as a dedication to Joe Santarpia, one of the band's sound engineers. Hot Wax was played for the first time since 2022-04-28 (50 show gap). I’m Not in Your Mind contained Alter Me teases and quotes. Cellophane contained Am I in Heaven? teases and Hot Wax quotes. Ambergris was introduced as a song about “the most expensive ingredient in modern humanity.” Iron Lung featured an extended Red Hot Chili Peppers style intro while Lucas was using the restroom, followed by additional mid-song banter about Lucas. A New World through Altered Beast II featured Leah Senior providing the narration.

Ambergris was introduced as a song about “the most expensive ingredient in modern humanity.” Iron Lung featured an extended Red Hot Chili Peppers style intro while Lucas was using the restroom, followed by additional mid-song banter about Lucas. A New World through Altered Beast II featured Leah Senior providing the narration.

Rattlesnake contained Sleep Drifter teases. The outro to Sleep Drifter contained Honey quotes before returning to the earlier Rattlesnake jam. Before Pleura, the band shouted out The Murlocs (with Cavs filling in on drums) for killing it prior to their set. Before Gaia, it was noted that Fishing for Fishies had been recorded by Gareth Liddiard of Tropical Fuck Storm. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. Before The Dripping Tap, Joey again shouted out The Murlocs, Leah Senior, and “Tropical Fuck Cunts”. KGLW.net Staff Notes: For those wondering about the nature of the show-opening Rattlesnake sequence, the band is jamming on Rattlesnake themes before an immediate and natural transition to Honey, which is immediately followed by a segue into Sleep Drifter that contains elements of Honey all the way through, and finally a return to the bridge and “Rattle, rattle, rattle” portion of the preceding Rattlesnake jam, thus completing the “Snake Sandwich”.

“Theme from Halloween” was played over the PA prior to the costumed band members walking on stage to a song played over the PA: "P.I.M.P" (50 Cent) for Ambrose, dressed as a pimp, "Mother" (Danzig) for Cavs, dressed as Glenn Danzig, "You've Got a Friend in Me" (Randy Newman) for Lucas, dressed as Buzz Lightyear, ""C" is for Cookie" (Sesame Street) for Cook, dressed as a cookie, with him throwing cookies into the crowd, "Oompa Loompa (#1)" (Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory) for Joey, dressed as an Oompa Loompa, and "Hollaback Girl" (Gwen Stefani) for Stu, dressed as a banana and throwing bananas into the crowd. Additionally, crew for the show were dressed as ketchup and mustard bottles. Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease. Alter Me was teased during the intro to People-Vultures. Mr. Beat contained a Persistence tease. Before Alter Me II, there was an extended discussion about bananas. Alter Me II through Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior (dressed as a witch) providing the narration. The verses were sung out of order in Altered Beast IV. Let Me Mend The Past contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote. Before Doom City, Stu denied a fan request for Catching Smoke saying that they didn’t know how to play it. Magenta Mountain featured a lyric change to reference Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory. Stu removed the banana costume prior to Self-Immolate and threw it into the crowd, citing that it was “too hot”. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over a metal vamp from the band in which Ambrose destroyed pumpkins that were on stage and threw the pieces into the crows. Poster by Jake Foreman

Vomit Coffin featured the now-infamous “Amby Snot Rocket”. Blame It on the Weather was humorously announced as the “complete opposite style of music” to the show-opening Murder of the Universe suite. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. Cut Throat Boogie was announced as a song about Ambrose getting stabbed in the neck with a broken bottle. Wah Wah began with the intro to The River and subsequently featured The River quotes and teases, as well as a jam. Sadie Sorceress featured an additional verse from The Grim Reaper played over the music to Sadie Sorceress. Joey requested the cameraperson zoom in to his face as close as possible during the intro to Self-Immolate and introduced Cavs as “John Henry Bonham”, followed by a drum solo and a “Moby Dick” (Led Zeppelin) tease, plus an additional brief drum solo and “Moby Dick” tease at the end of the song. Before Her and I (Slow Jam 2), the weather was discussed and the band’s ignorance regarding Fahrenheit to Celsius conversions was revealed. The song itself featured a Flamethrower jam, Slow Jam 1 and Magma teases, and a brief Cut Throat Boogie jam. Hot Wax was dedicated to one of the band's sound engineers, Joe Santarpia. Timeland was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Head On/Pill opened with “The Star-Spangled Banner” intro, contained Hypertension teases and a Witchcraft tease, and was unfinished leading into Am I in Heaven?. Venusian 1 closed with a Cavs drum solo which led into Venusian 2. Ambrose introduced Billabong Valley as being about a bushranger and is interrupted by Joey calling him “mad-cunt Morgan”. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was announced as a song about Ambrose’s somewhat troubled upbringing and was dedicated to Edith, Ambrose’s sister. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. Tetrachromacy and Hypertension were on the printed setlist but cut due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea Second Edition of Poster

Joey requested the cameraperson zoom in to his face as close as possible during the intro to Self-Immolate and introduced Cavs as “John Henry Bonham”, followed by a drum solo and a “Moby Dick” (Led Zeppelin) tease, plus an additional brief drum solo and “Moby Dick” tease at the end of the song. Before Her and I (Slow Jam 2), the weather was discussed and the band’s ignorance regarding Fahrenheit to Celsius conversions was revealed. The song itself featured a Flamethrower jam, Slow Jam 1 and Magma teases, and a brief Cut Throat Boogie jam. Hot Wax was dedicated to one of the band's sound engineers, Joe Santarpia. Timeland was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Head On/Pill opened with “The Star-Spangled Banner” intro, contained Hypertension teases and a Witchcraft tease, and was unfinished leading into Am I in Heaven?. Venusian 1 closed with a Cavs drum solo which led into Venusian 2. Ambrose introduced Billabong Valley as being about a bushranger and is interrupted by Joey calling him “mad-cunt Morgan”. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was announced as a song about Ambrose’s somewhat troubled upbringing and was dedicated to Edith, Ambrose’s sister. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. Tetrachromacy and Hypertension were on the printed setlist but cut due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea Second Edition of Poster

Venusian 1 closed with a Cavs drum solo which led into Venusian 2. Ambrose introduced Billabong Valley as being about a bushranger and is interrupted by Joey calling him “mad-cunt Morgan”. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was announced as a song about Ambrose’s somewhat troubled upbringing and was dedicated to Edith, Ambrose’s sister. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. Tetrachromacy and Hypertension were on the printed setlist but cut due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. Poster By Jason Galea Second Edition of Poster

Vomit Coffin featured the now-infamous “Amby Snot Rocket”. Blame It on the Weather was humorously announced as the “complete opposite style of music” to the show-opening Murder of the Universe suite. Work This Time featured a guitar solo. Cut Throat Boogie was announced as a song about Ambrose getting stabbed in the neck with a broken bottle. Wah Wah began with the intro to The River and subsequently featured The River quotes and teases, as well as a jam. Sadie Sorceress featured an additional verse from The Grim Reaper played over the music to Sadie Sorceress. Joey requested the cameraperson zoom in to his face as close as possible during the intro to Self-Immolate and introduced Cavs as “John Henry Bonham”, followed by a drum solo and a “Moby Dick” (Led Zeppelin) tease, plus an additional brief drum solo and “Moby Dick” tease at the end of the song. Before Her and I (Slow Jam 2), the weather was discussed and the band’s ignorance regarding Fahrenheit to Celsius conversions was revealed. The song itself featured a Flamethrower jam, Slow Jam 1 and Magma teases, and a brief Cut Throat Boogie jam. Hot Wax was dedicated to one of the band's sound engineers, Joe Santarpia. Timeland was played over the PA as the setbreak music. Head On/Pill opened with “The Star-Spangled Banner” intro, contained Hypertension teases and a Witchcraft tease, and was unfinished leading into Am I in Heaven?. Venusian 1 closed with a Cavs drum solo which led into Venusian 2. Ambrose introduced Billabong Valley as being about a bushranger and is interrupted by Joey calling him “mad-cunt Morgan”. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was announced as a song about Ambrose’s somewhat troubled upbringing and was dedicated to Edith, Ambrose’s sister. Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were performed with Leah Senior providing the narration. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. Tetrachromacy and Hypertension were on the printed setlist but cut due to time constraints. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live at Red Rocks ‘22. The band introduced the show by acknowledging how little they play hometown shows, citing Melbourne inner-city gentrification. Crumbling Castle was then introduced as “The Fourth Colour”, “Crumbling Castle”, and finally, “Gamma Knife”. The Fourth Colour featured a Cavs drum solo. The Grim Reaper was introduced as a song that Ambrose wrote about Stu, and featured an additional verse and quotes from Sadie Sorceress. Stu sang the third verse twice instead of the second verse in Ice V. Iron Lung was introduced as a “song about vaping”. Ambergris was introduced as a song about “the most expensive naturally occurring ingredient”. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Dragon jam.

This show was part of the 2022 Summer Haze Festival. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo over the top of a metal vamp from the band. Ice V contained Hypertension teases. Stu threw water bottles into the crowd during Hot Water. Amusing banter before The Dripping Tap referenced the heat, sweating, church, God, Satan, weed, and vaping. The Dripping Tap subsequently contained Hypertension teases and quotes. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The recording for this show is incomplete (missing Hypertension). Despite the incomplete recording, KGLW.net staff supports the -> transition from Ice V -> Hypertension based on available audio evidence of the start of the transition near the end of Ice V. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Despite the incomplete recording, KGLW.net staff supports the -> transition from Ice V -> Hypertension based on available audio evidence of the start of the transition near the end of Ice V. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

jamchart_notes: Lucas makes a rare assertive appearance in the second jam of this festival “Tap,” and Stu follows suit leading the band in a brief excursion into minor-key jamming that ultimately culminates in a neat “Hypertension” section, which spans both D minor and major as they work back to “drip drip.”

This show was part of the 2022 Rhythm & Alps Festival. “The End” (The Doors) was briefly quoted after The Fourth Colour. Am I In Heaven? contained Witchcraft, Head On/Pill and The Dripping Tap teases. Amusing banter followed Am I in Heaven? where the band talked about their New Year’s resolutions. Gaia featured a Cavs drum solo, during which Stu and Joey sprayed the crowd with champagne, followed by a full band “Moby Dick” (Led Zeppelin) tease. Boogieman Sam contained “My Babe” (Little Walter) and “Going Up the Country” (Canned Heat) quotes. Some other artists appearing at this festival were specifically shouted out by Joey: Marlon Williams, Sampa the Great, and Dope Lemon.
